full_prompt: Design one representative below-knee (transtibial) prosthetic pylon + pyramid adapter assembly, 30 mm diameter tubular pylon of nominal 250 mm clamped length between socket adapter and foot adapter, intended to be qualified to ISO 10328:2016 test level P5 (high activity / high body mass, up to 100 kg user with high-activity gait). The pylon connects a proximal male pyramid adapter (30 mm pyramid, standard prosthetic taper) to a distal female pyramid clamp adapter that interfaces to a foot. The part must pass two independent principal static ultimate strength tests per ISO 10328: Test Condition I (toe loading, heel-off phase) with an ultimate static load of 3360 N applied along the prescribed test-line offset, and Test Condition II (heel loading, heel-strike phase) with an ultimate static load of 4000 N applied along the prescribed test-line offset. Both tests use the ISO 10328 offset loading lines (Annex A) relative to the alignment reference, producing a combined axial + bending moment on the pylon cross-section. The pylon must additionally survive the P5 cyclic fatigue test: 3,000,000 cycles at the prescribed principal cyclic test forces (maximum cyclic force 1330 N for Test Condition I and 1265 N for Test Condition II per ISO 10328 P5, sinusoidal, with the specified minimum-to-maximum ratio) without initiation of a through-wall crack or loss of functional integrity. Target material is 6061-T6 aluminum alloy (yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa, E = 68.9 GPa) or equivalent; titanium Grade 5 is allowed. Geometry envelope: outer diameter 28-34 mm, wall thickness 1.5-3.0 mm for aluminum, total pylon length 200-400 mm adjustable by cut-to-length. Mass of the pylon-plus-adapters assembly must not exceed 400 g. Deliverable is a single CAD assembly (pyramid adapter + pylon tube + clamp adapter) suitable for (a) linear-elastic structural FEA under the two principal ultimate static loads, (b) fatigue life evaluation to 3e6 cycles using S-N data for the selected alloy. Vibration (modal), thermal, fluid, radiation analyses are NOT required. A fatigue (non-FEA static) solver is required to assess 3e6 cycle life.
short_prompt:
Design a below-knee (transtibial) prosthetic pylon assembly comprising a 30 mm tubular pylon between a proximal male pyramid adapter and a distal female pyramid clamp adapter, qualified to ISO 10328:2016 test level P5 (high-activity adult user up to 100 kg). Preferred material 6061-T6 aluminum; Ti-6Al-4V Grade 5 is allowed. Assembly mass <= 400 g.

Envelope: pylon OD 28-34 mm with 1.5-3.0 mm wall (aluminum), 200-400 mm cut-to-length, standard 30 mm prosthetic pyramid taper.

Load cases (ISO 10328 Annex A offset test lines, producing combined axial + bending).
- LC1 P5 static toe (Test Condition I): 3360 N ultimate static.
- LC2 P5 static heel (Test Condition II): 4000 N ultimate static.
- LC3 P5 cyclic toe: 1330 N peak, 3.0e6 cycles sinusoidal.
- LC4 P5 cyclic heel: 1265 N peak, 3.0e6 cycles sinusoidal.

Fatigue life is evaluated with an S-N fatigue solver; the static cases are linear-elastic FEA.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: ISO 10328 P5 mandates 3e6 cycle fatigue with no failure; requires S-N (or strain-life) fatigue evaluation beyond linear static FEA.

prompt.title: Transtibial pylon qualified to ISO 10328 P5
prompt.brief: Design a 30 mm tubular pylon + pyramid adapters passing ISO 10328 P5 static (toe 3360 N, heel 4000 N) and 3e6 cycle fatigue.

geometric_constraints.pyramid_adapter: 30 mm male prosthetic pyramid, standard taper

load_cases.name: P5_static_toe_TestConditionI
load_cases.direction: along ISO 10328 Annex A toe test line

load_cases.name: P5_static_heel_TestConditionII
load_cases.direction: along ISO 10328 Annex A heel test line

boundary_conditions.distal_load_point: load applied at offset per Annex A

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: yield strength of 6061-T6 under P5 ultimate static
pass_fail_criteria.note: Per ISO 10328, ultimate static test must be survived without failure; no plastic collapse at 3360/4000 N.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: clinical lightweight target for transtibial users

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: second-order tetrahedral (TET10) or hexahedral, refined at pyramid-tube interface

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Slender-column buckling pre-screened by section selection; not a prescribed ISO test.

uncertainty_flags.note: Exact numeric value taken from secondary summaries; confirm against ISO 10328:2016 Table 4 before use.

eval_coverage: assembly of 3 parts under single standard
